steps.title: Identify what the citation-tracking method accomplishes
steps.content: The passage presents tracking citation frequency as a way to [blank] a scholar's importance. The blank needs a verb describing what this tracking accomplishes—it measures or evaluates importance.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content: Assess means to evaluate something's value. Tracking citations determines a scholar's importance—this is what assessing does. The other choices don't fit: utilize (to use) and influence (to affect) don't denote evaluation, while undermine contradicts the passage by suggesting citations weaken rather than reveal significance. That matches choice D — D is correct.
